executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware wave affecting over 100 Canadian organizations in a single day, April 14, 2026. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit by two separate groups), Markham Stouffville Hospital, Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, and National Money Mart (database leaked). Twenty-one distinct ransomware groups are actively attacking Canadian targets across all sectors—healthcare, education, manufacturing, finance, transportation, and municipal government. The Qilin gang alone has claimed 18 Canadian victims today, while Clop has hit 13 organizations and Play has compromised 8. This represents a coordinated escalation targeting Canadian infrastructure with immediate operational and data breach risks.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 18 Canadian organizations including Eastern Townships School Board, Workers Health & Safety Centre, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), BioPharma Services, Waterloo Information Systems, Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Traffic Tech, A Lococo Wholesale, Gradient Wind Engineering, Bergmanis Preyra law firm, Pre-Con Builders, Questica, MG CPA, CST Coal, Watermark Beach Resort, Maheu&Maheu, Berts Electric
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application)

items.name: Clop
items.targets: 13 Canadian organizations including Strategic Objectives, Ideal Welders, Conwest, CMH Helicopters, Granite Pointe, SMT Lawyers, Cloud Data Works, Sensible Solutions, Whiskijack Resorts, CCCM.BC.CA, Technikel, Modtech, Eastplats, Centaur Products
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1048 (Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application)

items.name: Play
items.targets: 8 Canadian organizations including Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Paisley Products, Makivik, ALLMAX Nutrition, Pewarchuk CPA, Security ONE Alarm Systems, Select Tool
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1021 (Remote Services), T1003 (OS Credential Dumping)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is under severe, coordinated ransomware attack with over 100 organizations compromised on April 14, 2026. Critical infrastructure sectors affected include healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Peterborough Public Health, BioPharma Services), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Collège Supérieur De Montreal, Institut Teccart), municipal government (Municipality of North Perth), and telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network). No official CCCS advisories were identified in this feed.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose breached by two separate ransomware groups (CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters); Active Green + Ross and Kids & Company also compromised
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (Money Mart financial services) suffered database leak via Everest ransomware; Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi

report_2w.executive_summary: Canada's cybersecurity agency (CCCS) issued an alert for a critical Fortinet FortiClientEMS vulnerability (CVE-2026-35616) requiring immediate patching. The ShinyHunters threat actor claimed breaches of Rockstar Games via Anodot's Snowflake instance and Wynn Resorts affecting 21,000 employees. A critical remote code execution flaw in the widely-used Axios JavaScript library (CVE-2026-40175, CVSS 10.0) impacts OpenAI's macOS app and AWS Lambda services, requiring urgent updates. Iranian state-sponsored group MuddyWater is conducting espionage operations in the Middle East using Rust malware and Telegram infrastructure.

items.description: Axios promise-based HTTP client remote code execution vulnerability
items.software_affected: Axios library versions prior to 1.15.0, impacts OpenAI macOS app and AWS Lambda
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High - Remote code execution or complete system compromise possible

items.description: OpenClaw arbitrary code execution vulnerability in local plugin and hook installation
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Rockstar Games (via Anodot Snowflake breach), Wynn Resorts (21,000 employees affected), Abrigo Inc.
items.ttps: T1199 (Trusted Relationship - Snowflake supply chain), T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact - ransom demands). Threatening data leak on April 14 if ransom not paid.

items.name: Qilin (ransomware)
items.targets: Chalmers & Kubeck, Hofland, SAAM Towage (all unverified claims)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1486 (Ransomware), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service - leak sites)

items.name: MuddyWater (Iranian state-sponsored, APT)
items.targets: Middle East organizations
items.ttps: T1102 (Web Service - Telegram C2), T1027 (Obfuscated Files - Rust malware), T1566 (Phishing). Operation 'Olalampo' espionage campaign active since January 2026.

items.name: Handala (ransomware)
items.targets: Raz Zimmt (individual, chat logs leaked)
items.ttps: T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

items.name: North Korean threat actors (suspected)
items.targets: Axios npm package supply chain, OpenAI macOS app signing pipeline
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise - Software Supply Chain), T1195.001 (Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools). Compromised Axios npm library reached OpenAI production signing pipeline.

items.name: VENOM (phishing campaign)
items.targets: Senior executives, Microsoft logins
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1078 (Valid Accounts - credential theft)

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-007 on April 7 regarding CVE-2026-35616 affecting Fortinet FortiClientEMS, indicating active government concern over enterprise endpoint security. No specific breaches of major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were reported during this period. Multiple posts discuss Canadian domestic politics and general cybersecurity awareness, but no active incidents targeting Canadian critical infrastructure were identified.
